content:
In the early morning, Alishan.
The small train slowly enters the forest, with towering sacred trees on both sides. A sea of clouds surges beneath your feet, the sun slowly rising from the clouds, golden light spilling across the sea of clouds. Standing on the viewing platform, watching this spectacular scene, marveling at Alishan’s wonder.
This is Taiwan’s most classic scene.
Taiwan is a place that makes you nostalgic.
Province Profile#Location: Southeast China, Taiwan Island and surrounding islands Best Travel Season: March-May, September-November Highlights: Treasure Island scenery, night market cuisine, human warmth, seaside charm Must-Visit Attractions#Sun Moon Lake: Taiwan’s Pearl#Sun Moon Lake is Taiwan’s largest natural lake, one of “Taiwan’s Eight Scenes.”
The lake surface is divided by Lalu Island—the northern part is shaped like a sun, the southern part like a crescent moon, hence the name Sun Moon Lake. Xuanguang Temple and Wenwu Temple are lakeside attractions where you can overlook the lake. Ita Thao is a Thao people’s village where you can experience indigenous culture.
Best Experience: Cycle around the lake—this is one of CNN’s “Top 10 Most Beautiful Cycling Routes in the World.” See morning mist on the lake in the early morning, see sunset at dusk.
Alishan: Sea of Clouds and Sacred Trees#Alishan is Taiwan’s most famous scenic area, “Alishan Five Wonders”—sunrise, sea of clouds, sunset glow, forest, and railway.
Zhushan Sunrise Viewing Platform is the best place to see sunrise—take the small train up the mountain before dawn, watch the sun rise from the sea of clouds. Sacred Tree Group has dozens of thousand-year-old red cypresses, towering and shading the sky. Alishan Forest Railway is one of the world’s three great alpine railways—taking the small train through the forest is very vintage.
Best Experience: Stay overnight on Alishan, see sunrise and sea of clouds in the early morning. Take the small train to tour the forest, see the Sacred Tree Group.
Taipei: Prosperity and Earthly Vitality#Taipei is Taiwan’s premier city, with prosperity and earthly vitality coexisting.
Taipei 101 was once the world’s tallest building—climb to the top to overlook Taipei’s panorama. National Palace Museum houses the essence of Chinese cultural relics—the Jadeite Cabbage and Meat-Shaped Stone are the museum’s treasures. Chiang Kai-shek Memorial Hall is Taipei’s landmark—the guard changing ceremony on the hour is worth seeing.
Ximending is young people’s gathering place, with trends, food, and performances—extremely lively. Huashan 1914 Creative Park is an art district transformed from an old winery, with exhibitions, markets, and cafés—very artsy.
Best Experience: Visit the National Palace Museum during the day, wander night markets for food at night. Overlook Taipei’s night scenery from Taipei 101.
Kenting: Taiwan’s Hawaii#Kenting is Taiwan’s southernmost seaside resort area, “Taiwan’s Hawaii.”
Eluanbi Park has Taiwan’s southernmost lighthouse, with a white lighthouse standing against blue sky and sea. Longpan Park allows you to see the Pacific Ocean’s magnificent seascape. Baisha Beach is Kenting’s most beautiful beach, with white sand and clear water.
Kenting Street is Kenting’s liveliest place, with bars, restaurants, and guesthouses lining the street. In summer, young people party here.
Best Experience: Come to Kenting in summer, sunbathe on the beach, surf, play water sports. Drink and listen to music on Kenting Street at night.
Special Experiences#Night Market Cuisine#Taiwan night markets are food paradises, “If you haven’t been to a night market, you haven’t been to Taiwan.”
Shilin Night Market is Taipei’s largest night market, Ningxia Night Market is locals’ favorite, Fengjia Night Market is Taichung’s largest, Liouhe Night Market is Kaohsiung’s representative.
Must-Eat Foods: Sausage wrapped in sticky rice, stinky tofu, fried chicken cutlet, bubble tea, oyster omelet, braised pork rice, beef noodles… each one makes you drool.
Jiufen Old Street#Jiufen is an old street on the mountain, famous for “Spirited Away.”
Red lanterns, stone steps, and teahouses—very nostalgic. Drink tea in a teahouse, see the sea view below the mountain, feel Jiufen’s slow time.
Taroko Gorge#Taroko is Taiwan’s most beautiful gorge, with marble cliffs, clear streams, and primeval forests.
Swallow Grotto and Nine Turns Tunnel are the gorge’s essence—walking on the plank path, seeing cliffs and streams, thrilling and exciting.
Hualien Whale Watching#Hualien is Taiwan’s eastern secret, with Qingshui Cliffs, Qixingtan, and whale watching.
Take a boat out to sea, see dolphins and whales jumping in the Pacific Ocean—an unforgettable experience.
Travel Tips#Transportation: Taipei has an airport, with high-speed rail connecting Taipei, Taichung, and Kaohsiung. For round-island travel, recommend chartering a car or self-driving. Accommodation: In Taipei, stay near Ximending or the East District; in Kenting, stay on Kenting Street; in Hualien, stay in the city. Note: Many typhoons in Taiwan in summer—watch the weather. Night markets are crowded—watch your belongings. Respect local customs. Taiwan’s charm is hidden in those details.
The liveliness of night markets, the nostalgia of old streets, the beauty of lakes and mountains, the warmth of human connections… this treasure island always has reasons to make you nostalgic.
Come to Taiwan and feel the charm of the Treasure Island.
Travel China Series Complete#From Shanghai’s Magic City charm to Taiwan’s Treasure Island scenery, we’ve traveled through China’s 34 provinces in 34 days.
Every land has unique scenery; every place has moving stories.
China is vast, worth walking slowly and savoring carefully.
May you meet a better version of yourself on your journey.
Travel China series, complete.
title: Taiwan: Ten Thousand Charms of the Treasure Island, One Strait, Two Shores' Affection

content:
At dusk, Ruins of St. Paul’s.
The sunset dyes this four-hundred-year-old church ruin golden. Tourists take photos on the steps, pigeons flying in the square. Beside it, Lover’s Lane, with pink and yellow walls, is couples’ favorite photo spot. Standing before the ruins, imagining the church’s former glory, sighing at history’s vicissitudes.
This is Macau’s most iconic scene.
Macau is a small city full of surprises.
Province Profile#Location: Southern China, west side of the Pearl River Estuary Best Travel Season: October to following March Highlights: East-West fusion, World Heritage, Food Capital, Entertainment City Must-Visit Attractions#Ruins of St. Paul’s: Macau’s Landmark#The Ruins of St. Paul’s is Macau’s most famous attraction, a World Heritage site.
This church was built in 1602, and in 1835 a fire left only this wall. Exquisite stone carvings blend Chinese and Western artistic styles—a witness to Macau’s history.
Lover’s Lane is beside the Ruins of St. Paul’s, with pink and yellow walls—romantic beyond words. Mount Fortress is on the hilltop, where you can overlook Macau’s panorama.
Best Experience: Come to the Ruins of St. Paul’s at dusk, see the ruins in the sunset. At night when lights come on, it has a unique charm.
Macau Historic Centre: World Heritage#The Macau Historic Centre is China’s oldest existing Western architectural complex, a World Heritage site.
Senado Square is Macau’s central square, with black and white wave-patterned ground, Portuguese-style buildings on both sides. St. Dominic’s Church is Macau’s most beautiful church, Baroque style, bright yellow exterior. Leal Senado Building is a Portuguese-style building with exquisite interior decoration.
Best Experience: Wander through the historic centre, feel four hundred years of East-West fusion.
The Venetian: Oriental Romance#The Venetian is Macau’s largest integrated resort, themed after Venice.
Grand Canal Shoppes has an artificial sky, canals, and gondola boats—like being in Venice. Take a gondola, listen to the boatman sing Italian songs—romantic beyond words.
Best Experience: Stay overnight at The Venetian, feel the resort’s luxury. Wander the Grand Canal Shoppes at night, see the artificial sky change.
Coloane Island: Macau’s Back Garden#Coloane Island is Macau’s quietest place, preserving its original appearance.
Lord Stow’s Bakery is the birthplace of Portuguese egg tarts—worth queuing for. Hac Sa Beach is Macau’s only natural beach, with black sand. Seac Pai Van Country Park has pandas.
Best Experience: Ride bicycles on Coloane Island, eat Lord Stow’s egg tarts, feel Macau’s slow life.
Special Experiences#Macau Cuisine#Macau is a “UNESCO Creative City of Gastronomy,” with rich and diverse cuisine.
Portuguese Egg Tarts are Macau’s calling card, with crispy tart shells and tender custard—sweet and delicious. Lord Stow and Margaret’s Café e Nata are the most famous egg tart shops.
Pork Chop Bun is a Macau specialty, with crispy toasted bread and thick pork chop—one bite, satisfaction guaranteed. Tai Lei Loi Kei is the most famous pork chop bun shop.
Water Crab Porridge, Portuguese Chicken, and African Chicken are Macau specialties—you can eat authentic Portuguese cuisine at Portuguese restaurants.
Casino Experience#Macau is one of the world’s four great gambling cities, “Oriental Las Vegas.”
The Venetian, The Parisian, City of Dreams, and Wynn Macau are all large casino resorts. Even if you don’t gamble, you can wander inside and feel the luxurious atmosphere.
Macau Tower#Macau Tower is 338 meters high, Macau’s landmark.
Observation Deck allows you to overlook Macau’s panorama. Skywalk and Bungy Jump are games for the brave—jumping from 233 meters high, thrilling beyond words.
Travel Tips#Transportation: Macau has an international airport, or you can take a boat or the Hong Kong-Zhuhai-Macau Bridge from Hong Kong. City buses and taxis are convenient. Accommodation: Recommend staying at resorts like The Venetian or The Parisian to experience luxury. Or stay in the old city to feel history. Note: Casinos prohibit entry to those under 21. Egg tart shops have long queues—be patient. Macau’s charm lies in its diversity.
On one side, the four-hundred-year vicissitudes of the Ruins of St. Paul’s; on the other, The Venetian’s modern luxury; on one side, the old city’s earthly vitality; on the other, the casinos’ glitz and glamour.
Come to Macau and feel the charm of East-West fusion.
Tomorrow, we head to Taiwan to see the lake and mountain scenery of Sun Moon Lake and the night market cuisine of Taipei.
title: Macau: Four Hundred Years of East-West Fusion, Glittering City That Never Sleeps

content:
At dusk, Victoria Harbour.
The sunset dyes the skyline orange-red, lights in skyscrapers beginning to come on. On the Avenue of Stars, tourists look for celebrities’ handprints. At 8 PM, “A Symphony of Lights” begins—lights on buildings on both banks flash, accompanied by music, magnificent. Standing by the seaside in Tsim Sha Tsui, watching this brilliant scene, marveling at the Pearl of the Orient’s charm.
This is Hong Kong’s most classic scene.
Hong Kong is a city that makes you want to linger.
Province Profile#Location: Southern China, east side of the Pearl River Estuary Best Travel Season: October to following March Highlights: Shopping paradise, food capital, East meets West, international metropolis Must-Visit Attractions#Victoria Harbour: One of the World’s Three Great Natural Harbors#Victoria Harbour is Hong Kong’s soul, one of the world’s three great natural harbors.
Avenue of Stars has handprints of numerous celebrities—a must-visit spot for tourists. Star Ferry is Hong Kong’s oldest transportation—taking a boat across Victoria Harbour, seeing scenery on both banks, is Hong Kong people’s daily routine.
Best Experience: By the seaside in Tsim Sha Tsui at dusk, see sunset and night scenery. Take the Star Ferry, feel Hong Kong’s slow pace.
The Peak: Overlooking Hong Kong’s Panorama#The Peak is Hong Kong’s highest point, the best place to overlook Victoria Harbour’s panorama.
Peak Tram is the transportation up the mountain, with red trams running on steep tracks—very vintage. Sky Terrace 428 is the peak’s viewing platform, with 360-degree views of Hong Kong’s panorama. See skyscrapers during the day, see brilliant lights at night.
Best Experience: Go up the mountain at dusk, see sunset and night scenery. Take the tram down, feel the vintage atmosphere.
Disneyland: Fairy Tale World#Hong Kong Disneyland is China’s first Disneyland.
Cinderella Castle is the park’s symbol, Mystic Point, Adventureland, Fantasyland, and Tomorrowland each have their own character. At night there’s a fireworks show, with fireworks blooming over the castle—dreamlike.
Best Experience: Play all day, see the fireworks show. Stay overnight at a Disney hotel, feel the fairy tale world.
Ocean Park: Ocean and Amusement#Ocean Park is Hong Kong’s classic theme park.
Grand Aquarium allows you to see various marine life, Thrill Mountain has various exciting rides. Giant Panda Adventure has adorable giant pandas—a great place for family trips.
Special Experiences#Hong Kong-style Cuisine#Hong Kong is a food paradise, “Asia’s Food Capital.”
Tea Restaurants are Hong Kong people’s daily life—pineapple buns, milk tea, egg tarts, wonton noodles, each one classic. Tsui Wah Restaurant, Lan Fong Yuen, Australia Dairy Company are must-visit tea restaurants.
Dim Sum is the representative of Hong Kong-style morning tea—shrimp dumplings, siu mai, char siu bao, chicken feet. At Tim Ho Wan and One Dim Sum, you can eat authentic dim sum.
Roast Meats are Hong Kong specialties—roast goose, char siu, soy sauce chicken. Yung Kee Restaurant’s roast goose is Michelin-recommended.
Shopping Paradise#Hong Kong is a shopping paradise, a duty-free port, with goods cheaper than inland.
Harbour City is Asia’s largest shopping mall, Causeway Bay, Mong Kok, and Tsim Sha Tsui all have large malls. Ladies’ Market and Temple Street are open-air markets where you can find bargains.
Central and Lan Kwai Fong#Central is Hong Kong’s financial center, with skyscrapers—Hong Kong’s most prosperous place. Lan Kwai Fong is the gathering place for nightlife, with bars and restaurants lining the streets—extremely lively at night.
Tai O Fishing Village#Tai O is Hong Kong’s last fishing village, “Hong Kong’s Venice.”
Stilt houses are built on water, with fishing boats shuttling through. You can take a small boat tour, see Chinese white dolphins. Here preserves Hong Kong’s most primitive appearance.
Travel Tips#Transportation: Hong Kong has an international airport, high-speed rail directly to Shenzhen and Guangzhou. The city’s subway, buses, and trams are all convenient—recommend getting an Octopus card. Accommodation: Recommend staying in Tsim Sha Tsui or Causeway Bay, convenient transportation, convenient shopping. Note: Indoor air conditioning in Hong Kong is very cold—bring a jacket. Shop around when shopping, watch for authenticity. Hong Kong’s charm lies in its diversity.
On one side, skyscrapers’ modern metropolis; on the other, tea restaurants’ earthly vitality; on one side, Disneyland’s fairy tale world; on the other, Tai O’s fishing village charm.
Come to Hong Kong and feel the charm of East meeting West.
Tomorrow, we head to Macau to feel the glitz and glamour of the Oriental Las Vegas.
title: Hong Kong: Pearl of the Orient's Brilliant Night, East Meets West International City

content:
In winter, Snow Town.
Night falls, red lanterns light up, illuminating snow-covered small houses. Cooking smoke rises gently, the air filled with the scent of firewood. Walking on Xueyun Street, seeing those snow-covered houses, like mushroom houses in a fairy tale. Snowflakes fall, landing on shoulders, on eyelashes, in the heart.
This is China’s most beautiful snow scenery.
Heilongjiang is a place that makes dreams come true.
Province Profile#Location: Northernmost China, bordering Russia Best Travel Season: December-February (ice and snow), June-August (summer retreat) Highlights: Ice and snow fairy tale, China-Russia border, North Country scenery, Russian-style charm Must-Visit Attractions#Harbin: Oriental Moscow#Harbin is China’s most exotic city, “Oriental Moscow.”
Central Street is Harbin’s calling card, lined with European-style buildings, called an “Architecture Museum.” In winter, ice sculptures line both sides of the street—at night when lights come on, it’s magnificent. Buy a Madier popsicle, eat it on the street at minus 20 degrees—that’s Harbin people’s romance.
Saint Sophia Cathedral is Harbin’s landmark, a Byzantine-style building with a green dome, solemn and dignified. Ice and Snow World is the world’s largest ice and snow theme park, with ice sculptures, snow sculptures, and ice slides—a winter fairy tale world.
Sun Island is Harbin’s back garden—summer you can see squirrels and flowers, winter has the Snow Expo. Songhua River freezes in winter—you can skate, ride ice sleds, spin ice tops.
Best Experience: Come to Harbin in winter, wander Central Street, see Saint Sophia Cathedral, play at Ice and Snow World. Skate on the Songhua River, eat a Russian Western meal.
Snow Town: The Ceiling of China’s Snow Scenery#Snow Town is China’s most beautiful snow scenery, “China’s Snow Town.”
The snow here has high viscosity, falling on roofs, forming “snow mushrooms.” Red lanterns, wooden houses, white snow form a fairy tale-like picture. Xueyun Street is Snow Town’s main street, lined with various shops and guesthouses. Dream Home is Snow Town’s popular photo spot, with the most beautiful snow mushrooms.
Best Experience: Stay overnight in Snow Town, see the snow scenery without tourists in the early morning. At night, see red lanterns illuminating snow mushrooms, take a set of dreamlike photos.
Mohe: China’s Northernmost Point#Mohe is China’s northernmost city, “Arctic of China.”
Arctic Village is China’s northernmost village, where you can find the “Northernmost Post Office,” “Northernmost Sentry,” and “Northernmost Household.” In winter, you can see aurora here—the only place in China where aurora can be seen.
Best Experience: Come to Mohe in winter, see aurora, ride horse-drawn sleds, experience throwing boiling water that instantly freezes. Send a postcard from the “Northernmost Post Office,” stamped with “Arctic Village” postmark.
Heihe: China-Russia Border#Heihe faces Blagoveshchensk, Russia across the river, a China-Russia border city.
Standing by the river, you can clearly see the Russian city on the opposite bank. China-Russia Mutual Trade Zone sells Russian goods. Wudalianchi is a volcanic geopark, with volcanoes, mineral springs, and lava plateaus—a recuperation resort.
Special Experiences#Yabuli Skiing#Yabuli is China’s best ski resort, “China’s Ski Capital.”
Various levels of ski runs, from beginners to professionals can find suitable ones. Skiing, riding snow tubes, soaking in hot springs—the perfect combination for winter.
Zhalong Wetland#Zhalong Wetland is the world’s largest red-crowned crane breeding ground, “Crane Hometown.”
Come to Zhalong in summer, see red-crowned cranes flying and dancing in the wetland—elegant and beautiful.
Heilongjiang Cuisine#Heilongjiang cuisine is mainly Northeast dishes and Russian Western food. Guobaorou is a Harbin famous dish, crispy outside and tender inside, sweet and sour. Red Sausage is a Harbin specialty, Russian-style, eaten with da lie ba—perfect match. Demoli Stewed Fish is a Heilongjiang specialty, with tender fish and rich broth.
Russian Western Food is popular in Harbin—braised beef in pot, borscht, and cream mushroom soup are classics. Madier Popsicle is Harbin’s calling card, with rich milk flavor—most enjoyable eaten in winter.
Travel Tips#Transportation: Harbin is Heilongjiang’s transportation hub, with airport and high-speed rail. Mohe and Heihe both have airports. Accommodation: In Harbin, stay near Central Street; in Snow Town, stay in guesthouses within the scenic area; in Mohe, stay in Arctic Village. Note: Heilongjiang is extremely cold in winter—keep warm. Snow Town is crowded during peak season—book accommodation in advance. Seeing aurora requires luck—not every time you can see it. Heilongjiang’s charm lies in its ice and snow fairy tale.
Harbin’s ice and snow sculptures, Snow Town’s dreamlike snow scenery, Mohe’s aurora wonder… each place is a winter fairy tale.
Come to Heilongjiang and fulfill an ice and snow dream.
Tomorrow, we begin our Hong Kong-Macau-Taiwan journey, starting with Hong Kong to feel the charm of the Pearl of the Orient.
title: Heilongjiang: Ice and Snow Fairy Tale, North Country Scenery, Exotic Border Charm

content:
In winter, Wusong Island.
In the early morning, thin mist drifts on the Songhua River. Willow trees on both banks are draped in silver, like jade trees, crystal clear. Sunlight penetrates the thin mist, shining on the rime, sparkling. Walking on the island, you feel like you’re in a fairy tale world.
This is one of China’s four natural wonders.
Jilin is a place that amazes you.
Province Profile#Location: Central Northeast China, by the Songhua River Best Travel Season: December-February (rime), July-August (Changbai Mountain) Highlights: Changbai Heavenly Lake, rime wonder, Korean ethnic culture, film city Must-Visit Attractions#Changbai Mountain: Northeast’s First Sacred Mountain#Changbai Mountain is Northeast China’s highest peak, “Roof of Northeast,” the birthplace of the Manchu people.
Heavenly Lake is Changbai Mountain’s soul, China’s largest volcanic crater lake, 2,189 meters above sea level. The lake water is azure blue, reflecting the surrounding 16 peaks. Standing on the viewing platform, overlooking Heavenly Lake, marveling at nature’s wonder.
Changbai Waterfall flows from Heavenly Lake, with a 68-meter drop—magnificent in momentum. Underground Forest is a wonder formed after volcanic eruption, with primeval forest hidden in the crater. Green Deep Pool is Changbai Mountain’s most beautiful small waterfall, with emerald pool water and cascading waterfall—picturesque.
Best Experience: Come to Changbai Mountain in summer, see Heavenly Lake, see waterfalls, see flower seas. Come to Changbai Mountain in winter, ski, soak in hot springs, see snow scenery.
Wusong Island: Jade Trees and Silver Flowers#Wusong Island is China’s best place to view rime, one of “China’s Four Natural Wonders.”
Every December to the following February, water vapor from the Songhua River condenses in the cold air, forming rime. Willow trees on both banks are draped in silver, like jade trees, crystal clear.
Best Experience: Come to Wusong Island in the early morning, see rime at sunrise. When the sun rises, rime sparkles in the sunlight—breathtakingly beautiful.
Yanbian: Korean Ethnic Culture#Yanbian is China’s largest Korean ethnic settlement, with rich Korean ethnic culture.
Yanji is Yanbian’s capital, a paradise of Korean ethnic cuisine. Cold noodles, stone pot bibimbap, roast meat, rice cakes… everything makes you drool. Chinese Korean Ethnic Culture Park allows you to experience Korean ethnic culture, wear hanbok, perform farmer’s dance.
Fangchuan is where China, North Korea, and Russia meet, “One glance at three countries.” Standing on Longhu Pavilion, you can see North Korea across the Tumen River and Russia in the distance.
Best Experience: Eat authentic Korean ethnic cuisine in Yanji, wear hanbok for photos.
Changchun: Film City#Changchun is the cradle of New China’s film industry, “Film City.”
Changying Century City is China’s first world-class film theme park, where you can experience various film special effects. Changchun Film Studio is New China’s first film studio, where you can understand Chinese film history.
Puppet Emperor’s Palace Museum is Pu Yi’s “imperial palace,” understanding the history of the puppet Manchukuo. Jingyuetan is Changchun’s back garden, with forests, lakes, and ski slopes—a great place for leisure.
Special Experiences#Chagan Lake Winter Fishing#Chagan Lake Winter Fishing is the last fishing and hunting culture in northern China, “Fish Leaping from Ice Lake.”
Every winter, fishermen chisel holes, set nets, and pull nets on the ice—one net can catch hundreds of thousands of jin of fish. The scene is spectacular, a paradise for photography enthusiasts.
Goguryeo Sites#Ji’an Goguryeo Sites are a World Cultural Heritage, ruins of the Goguryeo Kingdom.
General’s Tomb is a Goguryeo king’s mausoleum, “Oriental Pyramid.” Haotai King Stele records Goguryeo’s history—precious material for studying Goguryeo.
Jilin Cuisine#Jilin cuisine is mainly Northeast dishes and Korean ethnic cuisine. Cold Noodles are a Korean ethnic specialty, with chewy noodles and sweet-sour soup—most refreshing in summer. Stone Pot Bibimbap is a Korean ethnic classic, with rice and various vegetables and chili sauce—spicy and delicious. Guobaorou and Little Chicken Stewed with Mushrooms are Northeast classics, large portions and affordable.
Jilin Ginseng, Antler, and Mink Fur are the “Three Treasures of Northeast”—good souvenirs.
Travel Tips#Transportation: Changchun is Jilin’s transportation hub, with airport and high-speed rail. Yanji and Changbai Mountain both have airports. Accommodation: At Changbai Mountain, stay in Erdaobaihe Town; on Wusong Island, stay in farmhouses on the island; in Yanji, stay in the city. Note: Changbai Mountain is at high altitude—keep warm. Wake up early to see rime—best time is early morning. Northeast is cold in winter—keep warm. Jilin’s beauty is hidden in those natural wonders.
The Heavenly Lake fairyland of Changbai Mountain, the jade trees and silver flowers of Wusong Island, the Korean ethnic culture of Yanbian… each place is nature’s gift.
Come to Jilin and feel the magnificence of the Northeast.
Tomorrow, we head to Heilongjiang to see the winter fairy tale of Ice and Snow World and the dreamlike scenery of Snow Town.
title: Jilin: Heavenly Lake's Beauty Under Changbai Mountain, Jade Trees and Silver Flowers on Wusong Island

content:
In summer, Dalian Xinghai Square.
The sea breeze carries the scent of salt, seagulls circling overhead. Some sunbathe on the beach, some play in the water, some cycle on the wooden boardwalk. In the distance, the cross-sea bridge spans the bay—magnificent and beautiful.
This is Northeast China’s most romantic seaside city.
Liaoning is a place with stories.
Province Profile#Location: Southern Northeast China, between Bohai Sea and Yellow Sea Best Travel Season: May-October Highlights: Qing Dynasty birthplace, seaside scenery, industrial heritage, Northeast cuisine Must-Visit Attractions#Shenyang Imperial Palace: Capital of Two Generations of Emperors#Shenyang Imperial Palace is one of China’s two existing palace architectural complexes, the imperial palace before the Qing Dynasty entered the pass.
Dazheng Hall was built by Nurhaci, an octagonal building blending Manchu, Mongolian, and Han styles. Chongzheng Hall was Huang Taiji’s office, solemn and dignified. Wensu Pavilion houses the “Complete Library of the Four Treasuries,” a royal library.
Compared to Beijing’s Forbidden City, Shenyang Imperial Palace is smaller but has more Manchu character. Walking through the palace, imagining Nurhaci and Huang Taiji strategizing here, feel the history of the Qing Dynasty’s rise.
Best Experience: Hire a guide to hear stories of the Qing Dynasty’s rise. Come to Shenyang Imperial Palace in winter, see the palace in snow—unique charm.
Dalian: Romantic City of the North#Dalian is one of China’s most romantic seaside cities, “Pearl of the North.”
Xinghai Square is Asia’s largest city square—you can see the sea, feed seagulls, fly kites. Binhai Road is Dalian’s most beautiful road, with mountains on one side and sea on the other—picturesque scenery along the way. Tiger Beach Ocean Park allows you to see polar animals and marine life—a great place for family trips.
Golden Pebble Beach has unique marine erosion landforms, “Dinosaur Exploring the Sea” and “Great Peng Spreading Wings”—lifelike. Discovery Kingdom is a large theme park, suitable for young people and families.
Best Experience: Come to Dalian in summer to escape heat, cycle on Binhai Road, watch sunset at Xinghai Square.
Panjin Red Beach: Nature’s Palette#Panjin Red Beach is the world’s largest reed wetland. Every autumn, seablite grass turns red, forming a red ocean.
Red beach, golden reeds, blue sky, white migratory birds form a colorful picture. Boardwalk is the best route to view Red Beach—you can take a boat through the reeds.
Best Experience: Come to Panjin in September-October, see Red Beach at its most spectacular moment.
Benxi Water Cave: Underground Wonder#Benxi Water Cave is the world’s largest water-filled karst cave, “Treasure of the North.”
Inside the cave, stalactites are in various shapes, with an underground river winding through. Take a boat through the cave, seeing all kinds of strangely shaped stalactites, marveling at nature’s uncanny workmanship.
Special Experiences#Zhang Xueliang’s Former Residence#Zhang Xueliang’s Former Residence is the official residence of Zhang Zuolin and Zhang Xueliang father and son, “Commander’s Mansion.”
A complex of buildings blending Chinese and Western styles—Daqing Building, Xiaoqing Building, and Zhao Si Missy’s Building—each with stories. Walking through the mansion, understand the legendary lives of the Zhang father and son.
Yalu River Broken Bridge#Dandong Yalu River Broken Bridge is a historical witness of the War to Resist US Aggression and Aid Korea.
The bridge was bombed by US forces, with only half remaining standing in the river. Standing on the broken bridge, seeing Sinuiju, North Korea on the opposite bank, feel the vicissitudes of history.
Liaoning Cuisine#Liaoning cuisine is mainly Northeast dishes, large portions and affordable. Guobaorou is a Northeast famous dish, crispy outside and tender inside, sweet and sour. Little Chicken Stewed with Mushrooms is a Northeast classic, with tender chicken and fragrant mushrooms. Sour Cabbage with White Meat is Northeast people’s home-style dish, with appetizing sour cabbage and fatty but not greasy white meat.
Dalian Seafood is even more famous—sea urchins, scallops, sea cucumbers, abalone—fresh and cheap. Shenyang Chicken Skeleton is Shenyang people’s favorite—roasted or stewed, both delicious.
Travel Tips#Transportation: Shenyang and Dalian both have airports and high-speed rail stations, convenient transportation. High-speed rail from Shenyang to Dalian takes about 1.5 hours. Accommodation: In Shenyang, stay near Zhongjie or Taiyuan Street; in Dalian, stay near Xinghai Square or Tiger Beach. Note: Northeast is cold in winter—keep warm. Dalian is crowded in summer—book accommodation in advance. Liaoning’s stories are hidden in those historical relics.
The Qing Dynasty relics of Shenyang Imperial Palace, the Republic era stories of Commander’s Mansion, the Korean War memories of Yalu River Broken Bridge… each place is a witness to history.
Come to Liaoning and understand the stories of the Northeast.
Tomorrow, we head to Jilin to see the Heavenly Lake of Changbai Mountain and the wonder of Wusong Island.
title: Liaoning: Birthplace of a Dynasty, Capital of Two Generations of Emperors

content:
In the early morning, Namtso Lake.
The first ray of sunlight falls on the snow mountain, golden light reflecting on the lake surface. The lake water is azure blue, reflecting the snow peaks of Nyainqêntanglha Mountains. Prayer flags flutter in the wind, mani piles stand quietly by the lake. A Tibetan grandmother turns prayer wheels by the lake, chanting the six-syllable mantra, steps faltering yet determined.
This is Tibet’s most sacred scene.
Tibet is a place that purifies the soul.
Province Profile#Location: Southwest China, Qinghai-Tibet Plateau Best Travel Season: May-October Highlights: Roof of the World, Tibetan Buddhism, snow mountains and sacred lakes, plateau scenery Must-Visit Attractions#Potala Palace: Snowy Holy Temple#Potala Palace is Tibet’s symbol, a World Cultural Heritage, a holy site of Tibetan Buddhism.
The palace is built against Red Mountain, 117 meters high, 13 stories, with 999 rooms. The Red Palace is the stupa hall of successive Dalai Lamas, the White Palace is where successive Dalai Lamas lived and worked.
Climb the palace, overlook Lhasa city, see devout believers prostrating on the prayer wheel path, feel the power of faith.
Best Experience: Reserve tickets in advance, enter as the first group in the morning. Hire a guide to hear the stories hidden in the palace.
Jokhang Temple and Barkhor Street: Lhasa’s Heart#Jokhang Temple is the most sacred temple of Tibetan Buddhism, “First Jokhang Temple, then Lhasa City.”
Inside the temple is enshrined the 12-year-old life-sized statue of Sakyamuni, brought by Princess Wencheng from Chang’an. Every day, countless believers prostrate before the temple—a shocking scene.
Barkhor Street is the prayer wheel path surrounding Jokhang Temple, Lhasa’s liveliest place. Lined with Tibetan-style buildings, shops selling thangkas, Tibetan incense, and turquoise. Follow believers turning prayer wheels, feel Lhasa’s earthly vitality.
Best Experience: Come to Barkhor Street in the early morning, see believers prostrating. In a sweet tea shop on the street, drink a cup of sweet tea, eat a bowl of Tibetan noodles, feel Lhasa people’s daily life.
Namtso Lake: Heavenly Lake Holy Realm#Namtso Lake is one of Tibet’s three sacred lakes, 4,718 meters above sea level, meaning “Heavenly Lake.”
The lake water is azure blue, reflecting the snow peaks of Nyainqêntanglha Mountains. Tashi Peninsula by the lake has caves, stone pillars, and prayer flags—the starting point for believers’ lake circumambulation.
Best Experience: Stay overnight by the lake, see sunset, sunrise, and the starry sky.
Mount Everest: Top of the World#Mount Everest is the world’s highest peak, 8,848.86 meters above sea level.
Everest Base Camp is the highest point ordinary people can reach, 5,200 meters above sea level. Here, you can look up at the top of the world from close range. At dusk, watch the sunset dye Everest golden; in the morning, watch sunrise illuminate the snow peak.
Best Experience: Stay overnight at base camp, see Everest’s sunset, sunrise, and starry sky. If you’re lucky, you might see banner clouds.
Special Experiences#Yamdrok Lake#Yamdrok Lake is one of Tibet’s three sacred lakes, “Coral Sea.”
The lake water presents a dreamlike blue-green color, like a giant sapphire. Standing at Gampala Pass, overlooking Yamdrok Lake—breathtakingly beautiful.
Nyingchi Peach Blossoms#Nyingchi is Tibet’s Jiangnan, at lower altitude with mild climate.
Every March-April, peach blossoms bloom, with pink and white flower seas contrasting with snow mountains and canyons. Gala Peach Blossom Village and Bomi Peach Blossom Valley are the best viewing spots.
Manasarovar and Mount Kailash#Manasarovar is the first of Tibet’s three sacred lakes, “Western Heavenly Pool.”
Mount Kailash is a sacred mountain shared by Tibetan Buddhism, Hinduism, and Bon, “Center of the World.” Circumambulating the mountain once can wash away a lifetime of sins.
Tibetan Cuisine#Tibetan cuisine is mainly yak meat and tsampa. Butter Tea is Tibetan people’s daily drink, salty and fragrant. Tsampa is the Tibetan staple, roasted barley flour with butter tea—simple but filling. Tibetan Noodles are Lhasa people’s breakfast, with rich soup and chewy noodles. Yak Meat is tender and delicious—dried yak meat is a good souvenir.
Travel Tips#Transportation: Lhasa has airport and train station—you can fly or take the Qinghai-Tibet Railway to Tibet. Tibet is vast—recommend chartering a car or joining a tour. Accommodation: In Lhasa, stay near Barkhor Street; at Namtso and Everest, stay in tents or prefab houses. Note: Tibet is at high altitude—watch for altitude sickness, take rhodiola in advance. Strong UV radiation—watch for sun protection. Respect local customs, don’t photograph believers and temples without permission. Tibet’s sacredness lies in the power of faith.
The solemnity of Potala Palace, the devotion at Jokhang Temple, the holiness of Namtso Lake, the majesty of Everest… each place makes you feel awe.
Come to Tibet, purify your soul, rediscover faith.
Tomorrow, we begin our Northeast journey, starting with Liaoning to see the Qing Dynasty relics at Shenyang Imperial Palace and the seaside scenery of Dalian.
title: Tibet: Faith on the Roof of the World, Holiness on the Snowy Plateau

content:
In the early morning, by Erhai Lake.
The first ray of sunlight falls on the lake surface, ripples of golden light. In the distance, Cangshan Mountain is shrouded in clouds and mist, snow on the peak appearing and disappearing. A Bai grandmother washes vegetables by the lake, several seagulls circling overhead. Rent an e-bike, ride along the lake-loop road, see sunrise, see fields, see Bai villages—feel Dali’s slow life.
This is the Dali that countless people dream of.
Yunnan is a place that makes you want to stay.
Province Profile#Location: Southwest China, western part of Yunnan-Guizhou Plateau Best Travel Season: Year-round, spring-like all seasons Highlights: Wind flowers snow moon, ethnic customs, tropical rainforest, alpine lakes Must-Visit Attractions#Dali: The Romance of Wind, Flowers, Snow, and Moon#Dali has four scenes of “wind, flowers, snow, and moon”—Xiaguan Wind, Shangguan Flowers, Cangshan Snow, Erhai Moon.
Erhai Lake is Dali’s soul, 130 kilometers around. Rent an e-bike or bicycle, ride along the lake-loop road, see sunrise, see fields, see Bai villages. Shuanglang is the most beautiful town by Erhai, facing the lake, backed by Cangshan—a great place to space out.
Dali Ancient City has over 600 years of history, with city walls, city gates, and old streets well-preserved. Renmin Road is the ancient city’s most artsy street, lined with cafés, bars, and craft shops. Yangren Street is where foreign tourists gather, full of exotic atmosphere.
Cangshan Mountain has 19 peaks and 18 streams—you can take the cable car up to see sea of clouds, see azaleas, overlook Erhai panorama.
Best Experience: Stay overnight by Erhai, see sunrise and sunset. Find a café in the ancient city, space out all afternoon.
Lijiang: The Ancient Town’s Day and Night#Lijiang Ancient Town is a World Cultural Heritage, “Oriental Venice.”
Sifang Street is the ancient town’s center, with flagstone paths, small bridges and flowing water, and Naxi architecture—every place is scenery. Mu Palace is the Naxi chieftain’s residence, “In the north, the Forbidden City; in the south, Mu Palace.” Lion Mountain offers a panoramic view of the ancient town—a great place to watch sunset.
At night, the ancient town is brilliantly lit, and bar street is extremely lively. Find a bar, listen to a folk song, drink a “Wind Flowers Snow Moon” beer, feel Lijiang’s night.
Jade Dragon Snow Mountain is Lijiang’s landmark, 5,596 meters above sea level, snow-capped year-round. Blue Moon Valley is a lake at the foot of the snow mountain, with azure water like a sapphire. Glacier Park allows close contact with snow mountain glaciers.
Best Experience: Wander the ancient town in the morning, avoid crowds. Listen to folk songs on bar street at night. Go up Jade Dragon Snow Mountain, see glaciers and Blue Moon Valley.
Shangri-La: The Sun and Moon in the Heart#Shangri-La is “the sun and moon in the heart,” paradise on earth.
Dukezong Ancient Town is China’s best-preserved Tibetan residential complex, with a giant prayer wheel standing on Guishan Mountain. Songzanlin Monastery is Yunnan’s largest Tibetan Buddhist monastery, “Little Potala Palace.” Pudacuo National Park has alpine lakes, primeval forests, and meadow pastures—picturesque scenery.
Best Experience: Stay overnight in Dukezong Ancient Town, turn prayer wheels in the morning, feel the tranquility of Tibetan areas.
Xishuangbanna: Tropical Rainforest Mystery#Xishuangbanna is China’s only tropical rainforest area, “Kingdom of Plants.”
Xishuangbanna Tropical Botanical Garden is China’s largest botanical garden, with various exotic flowers and plants. Wild Elephant Valley allows you to see wild Asian elephants. Dai Garden allows you to experience Dai culture—most lively during Water Splashing Festival.
Gaozhuang Xishuangjing is Xishuangbanna’s liveliest place, with night markets, bars, and restaurants. Starlight Night Market is Asia’s largest night market, with various foods and handicrafts—shop until your legs give out.
Best Experience: Come to Xishuangbanna in winter to escape cold, eat at the night market, explore the tropical rainforest.
Special Experiences#Yuanyang Rice Terraces#Yuanyang Rice Terraces are the masterpiece of Hani people’s generations of cultivation, a World Cultural Heritage.
Rice terraces spiral from foothills to mountaintops, like mirrors reflecting blue sky and white clouds. Most beautiful in winter when filled with water—at sunrise and sunset, light and shadow change, dreamlike.
Lugu Lake#Lugu Lake is the Mosuo people’s mother lake, “Oriental Kingdom of Daughters.”
The lake water is crystal clear—you can take a pig trough boat to tour the lake. Walking Marriage Bridge, Liwubi Island, and Goddess Bay—each place breathtakingly beautiful.
Yunnan Cuisine#Yunnan cuisine is rich and diverse. Crossing-the-Bridge Noodles is Yunnan’s calling card, with rich soup and plenty of ingredients. Steam Pot Chicken is a Yunnan famous dish, with tender chicken and rich broth. Flower Cakes are a Yunnan specialty, with rose filling—sweet and fragrant. Wild Mushrooms are a Yunnan delicacy—matsutake, termite mushrooms, and porcini, fresh and fragrant.
Travel Tips#Transportation: Kunming is Yunnan’s transportation hub, with airport and high-speed rail. Dali, Lijiang, Shangri-La, and Xishuangbanna all have airports. Accommodation: In Dali, stay by Erhai or in the ancient city; in Lijiang, stay in the ancient city or Shuhe; in Shangri-La, stay in Dukezong Ancient Town. Note: Yunnan has strong UV radiation—watch for sun protection. High-altitude areas—watch for altitude sickness. Lijiang and Dali have many tourists—recommend off-peak visits. Yunnan’s charm lies in its romance.
The wind, flowers, snow, and moon of Dali, the ancient town night scenery of Lijiang, the paradise on earth of Shangri-La, the tropical charm of Xishuangbanna… each place is poetry and distance.
Come to Yunnan and rediscover your romantic self.
Tomorrow, we head to Tibet to see the sacredness of Potala Palace and the majesty of Mount Everest.
title: Yunnan: South of Colorful Clouds, Wind Flowers Snow Moon, Poetry and Distance

content:
At dusk, Xijiang Thousand-Household Miao Village.
The sunset dyes the stilt houses golden, cooking smoke rising gently. Miao girls in full dress perform the Lusheng dance in the square. Night falls, lights of ten thousand homes come on, the entire village like a sea of stars. Standing on the viewing platform, watching this spectacular scene, marveling at the charm of Miao culture.
This is China’s largest Miao settlement village.
Guizhou is a place that amazes you.
Province Profile#Location: Southwest China, eastern part of Yunnan-Guizhou Plateau Best Travel Season: May-October Highlights: Hometown of waterfalls, ethnic customs, karst landforms, summer resort Must-Visit Attractions#Huangguoshu Waterfall: Asia’s Largest Waterfall#Huangguoshu Waterfall is Asia’s largest waterfall, 77.8 meters high, 101 meters wide.
Water cascades from a cliff, sound like thunder, water mist permeating. Water Curtain Cave is hidden behind the waterfall—you can feel the shock of the waterfall up close. Tianxingqiao is part of Huangguoshu scenic area, with water stone forests and Silver Chain Waterfall—another world.
Best Experience: Come to Huangguoshu in summer, when water volume is abundant and most spectacular. Walk through Water Curtain Cave, feel the shock of water cascading from above.
Xijiang Thousand-Household Miao Village: Living Fossil of Miao Culture#Xijiang Thousand-Household Miao Village is China’s largest Miao settlement village, with over 1,300 households.
Stilt houses are built against the mountain, layered and stacked—spectacular. Viewing Platform is the best place to see the village panorama—see morning mist at dawn, sunset at dusk, lights of ten thousand homes at night. Lusheng Ground is where Miao performances are held—you can watch Lusheng dance and Miao song and dance.
Best Experience: Stay in the village, eat a long-table banquet, drink “High Mountain Flowing Water” wine. Wake up early in the morning, see the village without tourists.
Libo Xiaoqikong: Emerald on Earth’s Belt#Libo Xiaoqikong is a World Natural Heritage, praised as the “Emerald on Earth’s Belt.”
Xiaoqikong Ancient Bridge is the scenic area’s symbol, built in the Qing Dynasty, a stone arch bridge spanning emerald green water. Water Forest is a unique landscape, with trees growing in water, roots intertwined. Wolong Tan is an emerald deep pool, with cascading waterfalls—beautiful beyond words.
Best Experience: Come to Xiaoqikong in summer, walk in the water, feel the coolness.
Fanjing Mountain: Maitreya’s Dojo, Sky City#Fanjing Mountain is a World Natural Heritage, a famous Buddhist mountain, Maitreya Bodhisattva’s dojo.
Red Clouds Golden Summit is Fanjing Mountain’s symbol, with two peaks connected by a bridge, like a sky city. Mushroom Stone is Fanjing Mountain’s wonder, a giant stone teetering yet standing for billions of years. Old Golden Summit is Fanjing Mountain’s highest point—you can see sunrise and sea of clouds.
Best Experience: Stay overnight on the mountain, watch sunrise and sea of clouds in the morning. If you’re lucky, you might see Buddha’s light.
Special Experiences#Zhaoxing Dong Village#Zhaoxing Dong Village is China’s largest Dong village, “First Dong Village.”
Drum Towers are the Dong village’s symbol, with five drum towers each unique. Wind and Rain Bridge spans the river, a masterpiece of Dong architecture. At night, you can listen to the Grand Song of the Dong People, feel the “Sounds of Heaven.”
Zhenyuan Ancient Town#Zhenyuan Ancient Town has over 2,000 years of history, “Oriental Venice.”
The Wuyang River flows through the town, with Ming and Qing buildings on both banks. Qinglong Cave ancient building complex is built against the mountain, hanging on cliffs—an architectural miracle.
Guizhou Cuisine#Guizhou cuisine is mainly sour and spicy. Sour Soup Fish is a Guizhou famous dish, with sour soup appetizing and fish tender. Changwang Noodles are a Guiyang snack, with blood curd, intestines, and noodles—numbing, spicy, and fragrant. Siwawa is a Guiyang specialty, thin pancakes wrapped with various vegetables, eaten dipped in sauce. Houttuynia is Guizhou people’s favorite—some love it, some hate it, must try.
Travel Tips#Transportation: Guiyang is Guizhou’s transportation hub, with airport and high-speed rail. Huangguoshu and Xijiang Miao Village both have direct buses. Accommodation: In Guiyang, stay in the city; in Xijiang Miao Village, stay within the scenic area; in Libo, stay near Xiaoqikong. Note: Guizhou has lots of rain—bring rain gear. Many mountain roads—prepare motion sickness medicine. In Miao and Dong villages, respect local customs. Guizhou’s beauty is hidden in those landscapes and villages.
The magnificence of Huangguoshu Waterfall, the charm of Xijiang Miao Village, the emerald waters of Xiaoqikong, the Buddha’s light of Fanjing Mountain… each place is nature’s gift.
Come to Guizhou and feel the charm of colorful ethnic customs.
Tomorrow, we head to Yunnan to see the wind, flowers, snow, and moon of Dali and the ancient town night scenery of Lijiang.
title: Guizhou: Landscape Gallery, Hometown of a Thousand Waterfalls, Colorful Ethnic Customs

content:
In autumn, Jiuzhaigou.
The lake water presents a dreamlike blue-green color, like gems embedded in the valley. Five Flower Lake’s colorful display, Nuorilang Waterfall’s cascading water, Long Lake’s deep tranquility… each place breathtakingly beautiful. Standing by the lake, watching the colorful forests reflected in the water, you can’t tell which is sky, which is water.
“After returning from Jiuzhaigou, no need to see other waters”—this is no exaggeration.
Sichuan is a place that makes you never want to leave.
Province Profile#Location: Southwest China, upper reaches of the Yangtze River Best Travel Season: September-November (Jiuzhaigou autumn colors), year-round (Chengdu) Highlights: Jiuzhaigou fairyland, panda hometown, Sichuan cuisine, Heavenly Kingdom Must-Visit Attractions#Jiuzhaigou: Fairyland on Earth#Jiuzhaigou is a World Natural Heritage, “Fairyland on Earth.”
Five Flower Lake is Jiuzhaigou’s most beautiful place, with lake water showing blue, green, yellow, and other colors like a palette. Nuorilang Waterfall is China’s widest alpine waterfall, 270 meters wide, magnificent in momentum. Long Lake is Jiuzhaigou’s largest lake, deep and tranquil. Pearl Shoal Waterfall is the filming location for “Journey to the West,” with water jumping on travertine beaches like countless pearls.
Best Experience: Come to Jiuzhaigou in autumn, see colorful forests reflected in the lake. Stay at the valley entrance, be the first to enter in the morning, avoid crowds.
Chengdu: A City You Come to and Never Want to Leave#Chengdu is China’s most leisurely city, “Don’t enter Sichuan when young, don’t leave Sichuan when old.”
Kuanzhai Alleys is Chengdu’s calling card, with old streets of blue bricks and gray tiles, lined with teahouses, snack shops, and creative stores. Find a teahouse, order a covered-bowl tea, get an ear cleaning, space out for an afternoon.
Jinli is Chengdu’s liveliest old street, with snacks, handicrafts, and Sichuan Opera performances—everything you need. Chunxi Road is Chengdu’s most bustling commercial street, with beauties everywhere, fashion and trends.
Giant Panda Breeding Research Base is the best place to see giant pandas—come in the morning, watch pandas eat bamboo, roll around, sleep, melting your heart.
Best Experience: Sit in a teahouse all afternoon, watch Chengdu people’s leisurely life. Eat hotpot at night, feel Chengdu’s fiery passion.
Mount Emei: Famous Buddhist Mountain#Mount Emei is one of China’s four famous Buddhist mountains, the bodhimanda of Samantabhadra Bodhisattva.
Golden Summit is Mount Emei’s highest point, 3,079 meters above sea level. In the early morning, watch sunrise, sea of clouds, and Buddha’s light—Mount Emei’s three wonders. Wannian Temple is Mount Emei’s oldest temple, with the beamless brick hall an architectural miracle.
Best Experience: Stay overnight on the mountain, watch sunrise in the morning. Walk down the mountain path, see the monkeys along the way.
Leshan Giant Buddha: World’s Largest Stone Buddha#The Leshan Giant Buddha is the world’s largest stone Buddha statue, 71 meters high, carved during the Tang Dynasty.
The Buddha is built against the mountain, with a benevolent face, overlooking the confluence of three rivers. You can take a boat to see the Buddha from a distance, or walk the plank path to see it up close.
Special Experiences#Daocheng Yading: The Last Shangri-La on the Blue Planet#Daocheng Yading is praised as “The Last Shangri-La on the Blue Planet.”
Three Sacred Mountains—Xiannairi, Yangmaiyong, and Xianuoduoji—are snow-capped year-round, sacred and solemn. Milk Lake and Five-Color Lake are alpine lakes with clear water reflecting snow mountains. Luorong Pasture is the starting point for hiking, with grasslands, streams, and snow mountains—picturesque scenery.
Best Experience: Come to Yading in autumn, see golden meadows and white snow mountains. Hike around the mountains, feel the sacredness of Tibetan areas.
Dujiangyan and Mount Qingcheng#Dujiangyan is a water conservancy project built by Li Bing and his son during the Warring States period, still in use after over 2,000 years, the originator of world water conservancy culture.
Mount Qingcheng is a famous Taoist mountain, “Mount Qingcheng is the most secluded under heaven.” Ancient trees tower in the mountains, with Taoist temples scattered—a great place to escape summer heat.
Sichuan Cuisine#Sichuan is a food paradise. Hotpot is Sichuan’s calling card, with red oil boiling, numbing and spicy. Mapo Tofu, Twice-Cooked Pork, and Kung Pao Chicken are Sichuan classics. Skewers, Maocai, and Bobo Chicken are street snacks—cheap and delicious.
Chengdu Snacks are even more abundant: Long Chao Shou, Zhong Dumplings, Dan Dan Noodles, Sweet Water Noodles, Three Cannon… each worth trying.
Travel Tips#Transportation: Chengdu is Sichuan’s transportation hub, with dual airports (Shuangliu, Tianfu), high-speed rail to all parts of the country. Jiuzhaigou has an airport, or take a bus from Chengdu. Accommodation: In Chengdu, stay near Chunxi Road or Kuanzhai Alleys; in Jiuzhaigou, stay at the valley entrance; on Mount Emei, stay on the mountain or at the foot. Note: Jiuzhaigou is at high altitude—watch for altitude sickness. Sichuan cuisine is numbing and spicy—proceed within your ability. Go early to see pandas—they’re active in the morning. Sichuan’s charm lies in its leisure.
Spacing out in a Chengdu teahouse, strolling by Jiuzhaigou’s lakes, watching adorable animals at the panda base, feasting in a hotpot restaurant… time here seems to pass especially slowly.
Come to Sichuan and learn to slow down.
Tomorrow, we head to Guizhou to see the magnificence of Huangguoshu Waterfall and the charm of Miao villages.
title: Sichuan: After Jiuzhaigou, No Need to See Other Waters; Heavenly Kingdom's Slow Life

content:
At dusk, Hongyadong.
Lights come on, with stilt houses layered like a golden castle. Standing on Qiansimen Bridge, watching Hongyadong reflect in the Jialing River, you feel like you’re in Hayao Miyazaki’s “Spirited Away.” Tourists hold up phones taking photos, marveling at this city’s magic.
This is Chongqing’s most popular scene.
Chongqing is a city where getting lost is a joy.
Province Profile#Location: Southwest China, upper reaches of the Yangtze River Best Travel Season: March-May, September-November Highlights: Mountain city magic, hotpot jianghu, Bayu culture, brilliant night scenery Must-Visit Attractions#Hongyadong: Real-Life Spirited Away#Hongyadong is Chongqing’s most representative attraction, a stilt house complex built against the mountain.
11-story building, layered and stacked, each floor with different scenery. Inside are snack streets, bar streets, and folk custom streets—wander and eat, and the afternoon passes.
Best Experience: Come to Hongyadong at dusk, wait for the lights to come on, and take a panoramic photo from Qiansimen Bridge.
Jiefangbei and Chaotianmen: The City’s Heart#Jiefangbei is Chongqing’s landmark, the War of Resistance Victory Monument. Surrounded by a bustling commercial district, with malls, restaurants, and snack shops.
Chaotianmen is where the Yangtze and Jialing Rivers meet, with two rivers flowing together, distinct in color. Raffles City is Chongqing’s new landmark, shaped like a ship setting sail—you can climb to the top to overlook the two rivers.
Best Experience: Shop on Jiefangbei at night, eat snacks. Then walk to Chaotianmen, see the two rivers’ night scenery.
Liziba Light Rail: Passing Through Buildings#Liziba Light Rail Station is Chongqing’s most magical existence, with Light Rail Line 2 passing through a residential building.
Standing on the viewing platform, watch the light rail train whistling by, entering the building—like a scene from a sci-fi movie. This is the ultimate expression of Chongqing’s “Mountain City” character.
Best Experience: Take Line 2, experience the light rail passing through a building.
Yangtze River Cableway: Aerial Bus#The Yangtze River Cableway is Chongqing’s unique transportation, connecting Yuzhong District and Nan’an District.
Sitting in the cabin, with the rolling Yangtze River beneath your feet, buildings of varying heights on both banks. This was once Chongqing people’s daily routine, now a must-experience item for tourists.
Best Experience: Take the cableway at dusk, see the lights on both banks.
Special Experiences#Ciqikou Ancient Town#Ciqikou is Chongqing’s best-preserved ancient town, “A flagstone road, a thousand years of Ciqikou.”
Flagstone paths are lined with time-honored shops—Chen Mahua, Maoxuewang, Ancient Town Chicken Intestines… eat your way through. In teahouses, you can listen to Sichuan Opera face-changing, feel Bayu culture.
Wulong Three Natural Bridges#Wulong Three Natural Bridges is a World Natural Heritage, filming location for “Transformers 4” and “Curse of the Golden Flower.”
Three natural stone bridges span the canyon, magnificent in momentum. Tianlong Bridge, Qinglong Bridge, and Heilong Bridge—each majestic and spectacular. At the bottom of the sinkhole is an ancient post station, as if traveling back to ancient times.
Dazu Rock Carvings#Dazu Rock Carvings is a World Cultural Heritage, representative of China’s late grotto art.
The carvings were made during the Tang and Song dynasties, with exquisite Buddha statues and rich themes. Baodingshan carvings are the essence, with Thousand-Hand Guanyin and Huayan Three Sages—each a precious work of art.
Chongqing Hotpot#In Chongqing, how can you not eat hotpot?
Old Hotpot is Chongqing people’s pride, with red oil boiling, numbing and spicy. Tripe, duck intestine, yellow throat, scorpion fish… dip in red oil, dip in garlic and sesame oil, one bite—numbing and spicy exploding on your tongue.
Recommended Shops: Peijie Old Hotpot, Zhu’s Fat Man Hotpot, Dongzi Old Hotpot… each has its own story.
Travel Tips#Transportation: Chongqing has an airport and multiple high-speed rail stations. The city’s rail transit is developed, but with many transfers—recommend using navigation. Accommodation: Recommend staying near Jiefangbei and Hongyadong, convenient transportation, beautiful night scenery. Note: Chongqing is a mountain city—lots of walking, wear comfortable shoes. Hotpot is very spicy—proceed within your ability. Chongqing’s charm lies in its magic.
You think you’re on the first floor, but you’re actually on the top floor; you think you’re on the top floor, but there’s still higher. This city always has corners that surprise you.
Come to Chongqing and feel the charm of an 8D magical city.
Tomorrow, we head to Sichuan to see the fairyland of Jiuzhaigou and the leisure of Chengdu.
title: Chongqing: Magical Mountain City of Fog, Fiery Hotpot Jianghu Passion

content:
In autumn, Kanas.
The lake water presents a dreamlike blue-green color, with white birch forests on both banks turning golden yellow, reflecting in the water. In the early morning, thin mist drifts on the lake surface—Immortal Bay, Moon Bay, and Dragon Bay, each place beautiful as a fairyland. Standing on Fish Viewing Platform, overlooking Kanas Lake, marveling at nature’s uncanny workmanship.
This is China’s most beautiful autumn scenery.
Xinjiang is a place that shocks you.
Province Profile#Location: Northwest China, China’s largest province by area Best Travel Season: June-September (Northern Xinjiang), September-October (Kanas autumn), year-round (Southern Xinjiang) Highlights: Tianshan scenery, Kanas fairyland, exotic charm, hometown of melons and fruits Must-Visit Attractions#Kanas: God’s Back Garden#Kanas is Xinjiang’s most beautiful scenic area, praised as “God’s Back Garden.”
Kanas Lake is China’s deepest alpine freshwater lake, with water changing colors with seasons. Three Bays—Immortal Bay, Moon Bay, and Dragon Bay—are Kanas’ essence, each place breathtakingly beautiful. Fish Viewing Platform is the best place to overlook Kanas Lake—requires climbing 1,068 steps.
Hemu Village is a Tuwa village, with log cabins scattered in the mountains, cooking smoke rising. In the early morning, mist shrouds the village like a fairy tale world. Baihaba Village is China’s northwesternmost village, bordering Kazakhstan, called the “First Village of the Northwest.”
Best Experience: Come to Kanas in autumn, stay overnight in Hemu Village, see the village in the morning mist. Space out by Kanas Lake, feel nature’s tranquility.
Sayram Lake: The Atlantic’s Last Tear#Sayram Lake is Xinjiang’s highest and largest alpine lake, called “The Atlantic’s Last Tear.”
The lake water is azure blue, like a giant sapphire. Surrounded by snow mountains and grasslands, with wild flowers blooming. The lake is 90 kilometers in circumference—every place is scenery.
Best Experience: Come to Sayram Lake in summer, see wild flowers blooming. Camp by the lake at dusk, watch sunset and starry sky.
Tianshan Heavenly Lake: Yaochi Fairyland#Tianshan Heavenly Lake is the legendary Yaochi of the Queen Mother of the West, 1,910 meters above sea level.
The lake water is clear, reflecting the snow peak of Bogda Peak. Surrounded by spruce forests and snow mountains—picturesque scenery. Take a boat tour of the lake, or hike through the forest, feel Tianshan’s magnificence.
Best Experience: Come to Heavenly Lake in summer to escape heat, come in winter to ski.
Kashgar: Western Region Charm#Kashgar is Xinjiang’s most exotic city, “Without visiting Kashgar, you haven’t been to Xinjiang.”
Kashgar Old City is the world’s largest earthen building complex, with maze-like alleys hiding century-old houses and handicraft workshops. Id Kah Mosque is China’s largest mosque, with golden dome shining in the sunlight. Apak Hoja Tomb is an Islamic saint’s mausoleum, said to house the Fragrant Concubine of Emperor Qianlong.
Kashgar Grand Bazaar is Central Asia’s largest market, with various goods dazzling. Century-Old Teahouse is Kashgar’s landmark—sit in the teahouse, drink a pot of milk tea, listen to Uyghur elders sing and play, feel the leisure of the Western Region.
Best Experience: Get lost in the old city, visit locals’ homes. Eat roast meat at the bazaar at night, watch Uyghur song and dance performances.
Special Experiences#Duku Highway#Duku Highway is one of China’s most beautiful highways, crossing Tianshan Mountains, connecting Dushanzi and Kuqa.
All the way through snow mountains, grasslands, canyons, and lakes—magnificent scenery. Nalati Grassland, Bayinbuluke Grassland, Big and Small Dragon Pools, Tianshan Mysterious Grand Canyon—each place worth stopping.
Best Experience: Self-drive Duku Highway, take 3-5 days to slowly appreciate the scenery along the way.
Taklamakan Desert#Taklamakan is China’s largest desert, the “Sea of Death.”
Desert Highway crosses the desert, 500 kilometers of asphalt road, with endless dunes on both sides. Luntai Poplar Forest is the world’s largest poplar forest, golden yellow in autumn.
Xinjiang Cuisine#Xinjiang cuisine is rich and diverse. Big Plate Chicken is a Xinjiang famous dish, with chicken, potatoes, and belt noodles—spicy and fragrant. Roasted Lamb Skewers are Xinjiang’s calling card, crispy outside and tender inside, fragrant aroma. Pilaf is a traditional Uyghur dish, with rice, lamb, and carrots—oily and delicious. Naan is a Xinjiang staple, fresh from the oven—crispy outside and soft inside, perfect with lamb skewers.
Xinjiang Fruits are world-famous: Turpan grapes, Hami melons, Korla fragrant pears, Aksu apples… bursting with sweetness.
Travel Tips#Transportation: Urumqi is Xinjiang’s transportation hub, with airport and high-speed rail. Xinjiang is vast—recommend self-driving or chartering cars. Duku Highway is open June-October each year. Accommodation: In Kanas, stay in Hemu Village or Jiadengyu; in Kashgar, stay in old city guesthouses; in Urumqi, stay in the city. Note: Xinjiang has a 2-hour time difference from inland—adjust your schedule. Strong UV radiation—watch for sun protection. Respect local ethnic customs. Xinjiang’s magnificence cannot be described in words.
The fairyland of Kanas, the blue of Sayram Lake, the snow peaks of Tianshan, the exotic charm of Kashgar… each place is nature’s masterpiece.
Come to Xinjiang and feel the vastness of heaven and earth.
Tomorrow, we begin our Southwest journey, starting with Chongqing to see the magic of the Mountain City and the fiery passion of hotpot.
title: Xinjiang: Different Scenery North and South of Tianshan, Western Region Charm Enters Dreams

content:
At dusk, Shapotou.
The Yellow River winds through the desert, the sunset dyeing the river water golden. A camel caravan slowly moves forward, camel bells ringing. Standing on the dunes, watching desert and Yellow River depend on each other, marveling at nature’s wonder.
This is the true portrayal of “Lonely smoke rises straight in the vast desert, the setting sun rounds the long river.”
Ningxia is a place that amazes you.
Province Profile#Location: Northwest China, upper reaches of the Yellow River Best Travel Season: May-October Highlights: Desert and Yellow River, Western Xia culture, North of the Great Wall, Hui ethnic culture Must-Visit Attractions#Shapotou: Where Desert Meets Yellow River#Shapotou is one of China’s most beautiful desert tourism areas, where the Yellow River and desert meet.
Yellow River Area allows you to take sheepskin raft drifting on the Yellow River, experiencing the ancient way of crossing the river. Desert Area allows you to ride camels, sand slide, drive off-road vehicles—experience the thrill of the desert. Standing on top of the dunes, overlooking the Yellow River winding through the desert—magnificent beyond words.
Best Experience: Climb the dunes at dusk, watch the sunset dye the Yellow River and desert golden. Watch the starry sky in the desert at night.
Western Xia Imperial Tombs: Mysterious Oriental Pyramids#The Western Xia Imperial Tombs are the tombs of Western Xia emperors, called the “Oriental Pyramids.”
9 imperial tombs, over 250 accompanying tombs, scattered at the foot of Helan Mountain. The tombs are built of rammed earth, conical in shape, having stood for a thousand years of wind and rain. Western Xia Museum displays Western Xia history and culture, with mysterious Western Xia script fascinating visitors.
Best Experience: Come to the tombs at dusk, watch the sunset dye the tombs golden, feel the vicissitudes of history.
Shahu Lake: North of the Great West#Shahu Lake is a lake in the desert, the representative of “North of the Great West.”
The lake water is clear, with reeds growing thick and migratory birds in flocks. Desert surrounds the lake, forming a unique landscape of sand and water together. Take a boat through the reeds, watch water birds fly, feel the beauty of Jiangnan.
Best Experience: Come to Shahu in spring to watch migratory bird migration. Come to Shahu in summer to view lotus flowers blooming.
Helan Mountain Rock Art: Ancient Memories#Helan Mountain Rock Art is an artistic heritage left by ancient humans 3,000-10,000 years ago.
The rock art is carved on cliffs, depicting animals, humans, hunting, and sacrifice scenes—precious material for studying ancient human life. Han Meilin Art Museum displays contemporary artist Han Meilin’s works inspired by rock art.
Best Experience: Hire a guide to hear the ancient stories hidden behind the rock art.
Special Experiences#Zhenbeipu Western Film City#Zhenbeipu Western Film City is one of China’s most famous film bases, where classic movies like “A Chinese Odyssey,” “Red Sorghum,” and “New Dragon Inn” were filmed.
Walking in the film city, you feel like traveling back to ancient times. You can rent a costume and take a set of martial arts photos.
Shuidonggou#Shuidonggou is China’s earliest excavated Paleolithic cultural site, praised as the “Birthplace of Chinese Prehistoric Archaeology.”
The site museum displays scenes of ancient human life. The Tunnel Warfare Caves are Ming Dynasty military defense works, with many mechanisms inside—mysterious and unpredictable.
Ningxia Cuisine#Ningxia cuisine is mainly beef and lamb. Hand-Grabbed Lamb is Ningxia’s calling card, tender and delicious. Lamb Offal Soup is Ningxia people’s breakfast, with rich soup and plenty of ingredients. Yellow River Carp has tender flesh, delicious whether braised or steamed. Zhongning Goji Berries are a Ningxia specialty, good for brewing tea or making soup.
Hui Ethnic Culture#Ningxia is a Hui Autonomous Region, with rich Hui ethnic culture.
Nanguan Mosque is Yinchuan’s landmark, with Arabic-style architecture, solemn and dignified. Hui Ethnic Street allows you to taste Hui cuisine and feel Hui ethnic culture.
Travel Tips#Transportation: Yinchuan is Ningxia’s transportation hub, with airport and high-speed rail. Shapotou is in Zhongwei City, which has a high-speed rail station. Accommodation: In Yinchuan, stay in the city; in Shapotou, stay in Zhongwei city or near the scenic area. Note: Ningxia is dry—drink plenty of water. Strong UV radiation in the desert—watch for sun protection. Ningxia’s uniqueness lies in the meeting of desert and Yellow River.
The magnificence of Shapotou, the mystery of Western Xia Imperial Tombs, the beauty of Shahu Lake, the antiquity of Helan Mountain rock art… each place is a gift from the Northwest land.
Come to Ningxia and feel the charm of North of the Great West.
Tomorrow, we head to Xinjiang to see the wind and snow of Tianshan Mountains, the fairyland of Kanas, and the exotic charm of Kashgar.
title: Ningxia: Beautiful Scenery North of the Great Wall, Desert and Yellow River in One Color

content:
In July, Menyuan.
Hundred-mile rapeseed flowers bloom, golden seas of flowers extending from foothills to mountaintops, contrasting with blue sky, white clouds, snow mountains, and grasslands. Bees dance among flowers, the air filled with floral fragrance. Standing on the viewing platform, seeing this golden ocean, your heart suddenly opens up.
This is China’s most beautiful rapeseed flower sea.
Qinghai is a place that purifies your soul.
Province Profile#Location: Northwest China, northeastern Qinghai-Tibet Plateau Best Travel Season: June-August (rapeseed flowers), July-August (Qinghai Lake) Highlights: Plateau lakes, sky mirror, rapeseed flower seas, source of three rivers Must-Visit Attractions#Qinghai Lake: Plateau Sapphire#Qinghai Lake is China’s largest saltwater lake, 4,500 square kilometers in area, 3,200 meters above sea level.
The lake water is azure blue, like a giant sapphire embedded in the plateau. Erlangjian Scenic Area is Qinghai Lake’s most developed area, where you can get close to the lake water. Heimahe is the best place to watch sunrise—in the early morning, the sun rises from the lake surface, golden light spreading.
Best Experience: Cycle around the lake, taking two days to slowly appreciate Qinghai Lake’s beauty. Or stay overnight by the lake, watch sunrise and sunset.
Chaka Salt Lake: Sky Mirror#Chaka Salt Lake is China’s “Sky Mirror,” rated by National Geographic as one of “55 places a person must visit in their lifetime.”
The lake surface is like a giant mirror, reflecting blue sky and white clouds. Walking on the lake surface, you feel like walking on clouds. Wearing a red dress, taking a set of reflection photos, is every girl’s dream.
Best Experience: Come at dusk, watch the sunset dye the sky and lake surface golden-red. Or come at night, watch the starry sky reflected on the lake surface.
Menyuan Rapeseed Flowers: Hundred-Mile Flower Sea#Menyuan’s rapeseed flower fields cover 600,000 mu, China’s largest rapeseed flower base.
Every July, rapeseed flowers bloom, from foothills to mountaintops, a sea of golden yellow. Yuanshan Flower Viewing Platform is the best viewing spot, overlooking the hundred-mile flower sea. Fangfen Bath allows you to walk into the flower sea, feeling the floral fragrance up close.
Best Experience: Come to Menyuan in mid-July, stay overnight, watch the golden flower sea at sunrise.
Kumbum Monastery: Tibetan Buddhist Holy Site#Kumbum Monastery is one of the six great monasteries of the Gelug school of Tibetan Buddhism, the birthplace of Tsongkhapa.
Great Golden Tile Hall is Kumbum’s core, with golden roofs shining in the sunlight. Eight Treasure Auspicious Pagoda is Kumbum’s symbol, with eight white pagodas lined up. Butter Flower, Murals, and Pile Embroidery are Kumbum’s “Three Wonders,” exquisite beyond compare.
Best Experience: Hire a guide to understand Tibetan Buddhist culture. Turn a prayer wheel, pray for your family.
Special Experiences#Emerald Lake#Emerald Lake is a salt lake in Dachaidan, named because the lake water appears emerald green.
Smaller and more pristine than Chaka Salt Lake. The lake water is clear, reflecting blue sky, white clouds, and distant snow mountains—beautiful as a painting.
Water Yadan#Water Yadan is the world’s only water Yadan landform.
Yadan earth mounds are scattered in the lake water, in various shapes, like castles. At sunrise and sunset, light and shadow change—dreamlike.
Qilian Mountain Grassland#Qilian Mountain Grassland is one of China’s most beautiful grasslands, “Under Qilian Mountains, cattle and sheep are fat.”
Grasslands, snow mountains, forests, and rivers form a magnificent picture. Ride horses across the grassland, feel the vastness of the plateau.
Qinghai Cuisine#Qinghai cuisine is mainly beef, mutton, and noodles. Hand-Grabbed Lamb is tender and delicious, eaten dipped in salt and pepper. Niangpi is a Qinghai specialty, chewy and smooth. Yogurt is rich and mellow, sprinkled with white sugar—sweet and sour. Highland Barley Wine is the Tibetan people’s hospitality drink, not high in alcohol, mellow taste.
Travel Tips#Transportation: Xining is Qinghai’s transportation hub, with airport and high-speed rail. For Qinghai Lake and Chaka Salt Lake, recommend chartering a car or self-driving. Accommodation: For Qinghai Lake, stay near Heimahe or Erlangjian; for Chaka Salt Lake, stay in Chaka Town; in Xining, stay in the city. Note: Qinghai is at high altitude—watch for altitude sickness. Large temperature difference between day and night—even in summer, bring a jacket. Strong UV radiation—watch for sun protection. Qinghai’s purity purifies the soul.
The blue waves of Qinghai Lake, the reflection of Chaka Salt Lake, the rapeseed flower sea of Menyuan, the grasslands of Qilian Mountains… each place is nature’s gift.
Come to Qinghai and rediscover your inner peace.
Tomorrow, we head to Ningxia to see the desert and Yellow River at Shapotou and the mystery of Western Xia Imperial Tombs.
title: Qinghai: Dark Clouds Over Qinghai's Snow Mountains, Sky Mirror Reflects the World

content:
At dusk, Mingsha Mountain.
The sunset dyes the desert golden, a camel caravan slowly moving forward, camel bells ringing. In the distance, Crescent Moon Spring lies quietly in the desert like a crescent moon. Climb the dunes, watch the sunset dye the sky orange-red—in that moment, you’ll understand what “Lonely smoke rises straight in the vast desert, the setting sun rounds the long river” means.
This is the most beautiful scene on the Silk Road.
Gansu is a place that amazes you.
Province Profile#Location: Northwest China, upper reaches of the Yellow River Best Travel Season: May-October Highlights: Silk Road, Dunhuang art, Danxia wonder, Hexi Corridor Must-Visit Attractions#Dunhuang Mogao Grottoes: Oriental Art Treasury#Mogao Grottoes is the world’s largest existing Buddhist art treasury, with 735 caves, 45,000 square meters of murals, and 2,415 painted sculptures.
Cave 96 has Mogao’s largest Buddha statue, 35.5 meters high—Mogao’s symbol. Cave 17 is the Library Cave, where over 50,000 ancient documents were discovered, shocking the world. Cave 45’s Bodhisattva statue is praised as the “Oriental Venus,” graceful and elegant.
Best Experience: Reserve Class A tickets in advance to see 8 caves and two digital films. Hire a guide to hear the stories hidden behind the murals.
Mingsha Mountain and Crescent Moon Spring: Desert Wonder#Mingsha Mountain is named because the sand “sings,” Crescent Moon Spring is named because it’s shaped like a crescent moon.
For thousands of years, Crescent Moon Spring has never dried up in the desert, called the “First Spring in the Desert.” Climb Mingsha Mountain, overlook Crescent Moon Spring, see desert and spring water depend on each other, marvel at nature’s wonder.
Best Experience: Ride camels up the mountain at dusk, watch the desert sunset. Watch the starry sky in the desert at night.
Zhangye Danxia: Seven-Color Danxia#Zhangye Danxia is one of China’s most beautiful Danxia landforms, called “God’s Spilled Palette.”
On red sandstone, colors of red, yellow, orange, green, white, gray-green, and gray-black appear, layered like a dream. Seven-Color Danxia Scenic Area has four viewing platforms—each angle is a painting.
Best Experience: Come at dusk, watch the sunset dye the Danxia golden-red, colors even more brilliant.
Jiayuguan: The First Majestic Pass Under Heaven#Jiayuguan is the westernmost pass of the Ming Great Wall, “The First Majestic Pass Under Heaven.”
The pass city was built between Qilian Mountain and Heishan Mountain, the throat of the Silk Road. Climb the city tower, see the vast Gobi, imagine the scene of camel caravans passing through, feel the desolation of “West of Yangguan, no old friends.”
Best Experience: Climb the city tower at dusk, watch the sunset dye the pass golden, feel the majesty of the frontier.
Special Experiences#Maijishan Grottoes#Maijishan Grottoes is one of China’s four great grottoes, famous for clay sculptures.
The grottoes were carved on cliffs, with plank paths hanging in the air—thrilling. Cave 4’s “Flower Scattering Tower” is Maijishan’s essence, with exquisite murals and vivid sculptures.
Labrang Monastery#Labrang Monastery is one of the six great monasteries of the Gelug school of Tibetan Buddhism, called the “World Tibetan Buddhist Academy.”
Red walls and golden roofs, prayer flags fluttering. Walk through the prayer wheel corridor, listen to monks chanting, feel the solemnity of Tibetan Buddhism.
Yellow River Stone Forest#Yellow River Stone Forest is a wonder by the Yellow River, with stone pillars standing in various shapes.
Take a sheepskin raft drifting on the Yellow River, take a donkey cart through the stone forest, experience the ruggedness of the Northwest.
Gansu Cuisine#Gansu cuisine is mainly noodles and lamb. Lanzhou Beef Noodles is Gansu’s calling card, “One clear, two white, three red, four green, five yellow”—clear soup, chewy noodles. Hand-Grabbed Lamb is tender and delicious, eaten dipped in salt and pepper. Niangpi is a type of liangpi, chewy and smooth.
Travel Tips#Transportation: Lanzhou is Gansu’s transportation hub, with airport and high-speed rail. Dunhuang and Zhangye both have airports and high-speed rail stations. Hexi Corridor attractions are scattered—recommend chartering a car or self-driving. Accommodation: In Dunhuang, stay near Mingsha Mountain; in Zhangye, stay near Danxia scenic area; in Lanzhou, stay in the city. Note: Mogao Grottoes require advance reservation—tickets are hard to get during peak season. The Northwest is dry—drink plenty of water, watch for sun protection. Gansu’s magnificence is hidden in those Silk Road relics.
The art treasury of Mogao Grottoes, the desert wonder of Mingsha Mountain, the colorful wonder of Zhangye Danxia, the majestic pass of Jiayuguan… each place is nature’s masterpiece.
Come to Gansu and retrace the Silk Road.
Tomorrow, we head to Qinghai to see the blue waves of Qinghai Lake and the sky mirror of Chaka Salt Lake.
title: Gansu: Silk Road Pearl Dunhuang Dream, Seven-Color Danxia Falls to Earth

content:
At dusk, Xi’an City Wall.
